tasks.title: Fix Dockerfile to serve on port 8080
tasks.description: Update the Dockerfile so nginx listens on port 8080 instead of 80, and update the EXPOSE directive accordingly.
tasks.acceptance_criteria: The Dockerfile builds successfully and the containerized app listens on port 8080.
tasks.claudeMd:
# Project Context

You are fixing a Dockerfile for a static HTML/CSS/JS game served by nginx:alpine.

## What to change

Edit `Dockerfile` (project root) to:
1. Add a `RUN sed -i 's/listen\s*80;/listen 8080;/g' /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf` line before the EXPOSE directive
2. Change `EXPOSE 80` to `EXPOSE 8080`

That's it — two lines changed in one file.

## Verification

After editing, run:
```bash
docker build -t test-app . && docker run --rm -d -p 8080:8080 --name test-container test-app && sleep 2 && curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:8080 && docker stop test-container
```
Expect HTTP 200.

## Conventions
- Keep the Dockerfile minimal — no multi-stage build, no custom nginx.conf file
- Use `sed` inline replacement to avoid adding extra files
- Do not modify any other files

checklist.description: Add sed command to change nginx listen port from 80 to 8080
steps: Edit Dockerfile to add RUN sed -i 's/listen\s*80;/listen 8080;/g' /etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf before the EXPOSE line
Verify the Dockerfile syntax looks correct

checklist.description: Docker build succeeds and app responds on port 8080

quality_checklist.description: Dockerfile is minimal and only changes what's necessary
steps: Verify no unnecessary layers or files were added
Verify only the listen port and EXPOSE directive changed

checks.description: App responds with HTTP 200 on port 8080
steps: Build the Docker image: docker build -t test-app .
Run the container: docker run --rm -d -p 8080:8080 --name test-container test-app
Wait 2 seconds for nginx to start
Stop the container: docker stop test-container
